title: Employee Prisma Schema Models
description: Create Prisma schema models for Employee and EmployeeID. Employee mirrors SIS data (sisEmployeeId, fullName, jobTitle, email, status). EmployeeID stores local metadata (photoUrl, expirationDate, createdAt, updatedAt).
tests: Schema file validates with Prisma
Migration creates tables correctly
Relations between Employee and EmployeeID are properly defined
Required fields have appropriate constraints

title: FACTS SIS Employee Sync Service
description: Create a service module to fetch employee data from FACTS SIS REST API. Includes authentication, error handling, and data transformation to match Employee schema.
tests: Service successfully authenticates with FACTS API
Service fetches employee list and transforms data correctly
Service handles API errors gracefully
Service validates required fields from SIS response

title: Employee Sync Background Job
description: Implement a scheduled background job (daily/hourly) that syncs employee data from FACTS SIS to local database. Updates existing employees and creates new ones based on SIS status.
tests: Job successfully runs on schedule
New employees from SIS are created in database
Existing employees are updated with latest SIS data
Inactive employees have status updated correctly
Job handles failures without crashing

title: Admin Employee List View
description: Create an admin route that displays a list of all employees with their status, expiration dates, and photo status. Includes search/filter capabilities.
tests: Admin can view list of all employees
List displays employee name, job title, status, and expiration date
Search filters employees by name or email
Filter by status (active/inactive) works correctly
Non-admin users cannot access this route

title: Employee ID Route (Own ID View)
description: Create a route for employees to view their own ID. Displays employee information and provides download button. Employees can only access their own ID.
tests: Employee can view their own ID page
Employee cannot view other employees' IDs
Page displays employee name, title, photo, and expiration date
Download button is visible and functional
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login

title: Admin Photo Upload
description: Implement photo upload functionality for admins. Admins can upload/replace employee photos. Photos are stored in file storage and URLs saved to EmployeeID record.
tests: Admin can upload photo for any employee
Uploaded photo replaces existing photo if present
Photo is validated (format, size)
Photo URL is saved to EmployeeID record
Non-admin users cannot upload photos
Error handling for invalid files works correctly

title: Expiration Date Management
description: Implement expiration date management. Default expiration is July 1 of current school year. Admins can view and override expiration dates for any employee.
tests: Default expiration date is set to July 1 of current school year
Admin can view expiration date for any employee
Admin can update expiration date
Expiration date is stored correctly in database
Date validation prevents invalid dates
Employees can see their own expiration date

title: QR Code Generation Service
description: Create a service that generates QR codes for employee IDs. QR code links to public verification URL: /verify/:employeeId. Service handles error cases and returns base64 or buffer.
tests: Service generates valid QR code
QR code contains correct verification URL format
QR code is readable and scans correctly
Service handles invalid employee IDs gracefully
QR code size and format are appropriate for PDF embedding

title: PDF ID Card Generation
description: Implement PDF generation for employee IDs using @react-pdf/renderer. ID includes front (name, title, photo, expiration, logo) and back (QR code). Wallet-size format with proper branding.
tests: PDF generates successfully for valid employee
PDF includes all required fields (name, title, photo, expiration, logo)
PDF includes QR code on back
PDF is wallet-sized and printable
School branding (logo, colors) is correctly applied
Error handling for missing photo or data works correctly

title: PDF Download Endpoint
description: Create server endpoint/loader that generates and streams PDF ID card for download. Employees can download their own PDF, admins can download any employee's PDF.
tests: Employee can download their own PDF ID
Employee cannot download other employees' PDFs
Admin can download any employee's PDF
PDF is generated and streamed correctly
Content-Type header is set to application/pdf
Download works with valid authentication

title: Public Verification Route
description: Create public (unauthenticated) route at /verify/:employeeId that displays employee verification status. Shows name, title, status (active/inactive), expiration, and validity badge.
tests: Verification page is publicly accessible without authentication
Page displays employee name and job title
Page shows active/inactive status correctly
Page displays expiration date
Page shows valid/invalid badge based on status and expiration
Invalid employee IDs show appropriate error message
Page includes school branding

title: Verification Status Logic
description: Implement business logic to determine if an ID is valid: active status from SIS AND current date <= expiration date. Invalid otherwise. Logic used by verification page.
tests: Valid status returned for active employee with future expiration
Invalid status returned for inactive employee
Invalid status returned for expired ID (past expiration date)
Invalid status returned for employee missing from SIS
Status calculation uses current date correctly

title: Google OAuth Integration for Employees
description: Integrate Google OAuth authentication with school email domain restriction. Only users with school email domain can authenticate. On first login, create Employee record if user exists in SIS.
tests: Google OAuth authentication works correctly
Only school email domain users can authenticate
Non-school email users are rejected
First-time employee login creates Employee record if in SIS
Returning employees can log in successfully
Session is created and maintained correctly

title: Admin Role Management
description: Implement admin role assignment system. Admins are identified by a flag in User model or separate Admin model. Admin permissions enforced across all admin routes.
tests: Admin users can access admin routes
Non-admin users are denied access to admin routes
Admin flag/role is stored and retrieved correctly
Permission checks work in route loaders/actions
Admin users can perform admin actions (upload photos, manage IDs)

title: Employee ID Creation on First View
description: When an employee first views their ID (or admin views employee ID), automatically create EmployeeID record if it doesn't exist. Set default expiration date (July 1) if not set.
tests: EmployeeID record created when employee first views their ID
Default expiration date set to July 1 if not provided
Record creation happens automatically without admin intervention
Subsequent views don't recreate the record
Admin viewing employee ID triggers creation if missing

title: Photo Storage Integration
description: Implement file storage integration for employee photos. Use Fly.io volume or S3-compatible storage. Store photos with unique keys and generate URLs for database storage.
tests: Photos are uploaded to configured storage backend
Photos are stored with unique, secure keys
Photo URLs are generated and stored in database
Photos can be retrieved by URL
Storage handles errors gracefully (full disk, permissions, etc.)

title: Admin Employee Detail View
description: Create admin route to view individual employee details including SIS sync status, photo, expiration date, and actions (upload photo, update expiration, view/download ID).
tests: Admin can view employee detail page
Page displays all employee information
Page shows photo upload interface
Page shows expiration date editing
Page includes download/view ID button
Non-admin users cannot access employee detail pages

title: School Branding Configuration
description: Create configuration system for school branding (logo URL, colors, name). Branding applied to PDF IDs and verification pages. Configuration stored in environment variables or database.
tests: School logo is displayed on PDF IDs
School colors are applied correctly
School name appears on ID and verification pages
Branding configuration can be updated
Default branding is used if not configured

title: Verification Page SEO and Metadata
description: Add proper SEO metadata to verification page including title, description, and Open Graph tags. Ensures verification pages are shareable and indexable appropriately.
tests: Verification page has appropriate title tag
Page includes meta description
Open Graph tags are present and correct
Page title includes employee name and status
Metadata updates based on verification status

title: Error Handling and User Feedback
description: Implement comprehensive error handling for ID generation, photo uploads, and verification. Display user-friendly error messages. Log errors for debugging.
tests: Missing employee data shows appropriate error message
Photo upload errors are displayed to user
PDF generation errors are handled gracefully
Verification errors show clear messages
Errors are logged for debugging
Users see helpful feedback messages for all actions

title: Rate Limiting on Verification Endpoint
description: Implement rate limiting on public verification endpoint to prevent abuse and scraping. Use express-rate-limit or similar. Allow reasonable access while blocking excessive requests.
tests: Verification endpoint enforces rate limits
Normal usage is not blocked by rate limits
Excessive requests are rate-limited correctly
Rate limit errors return appropriate status codes
Rate limits are configurable
Rate limiting doesn't affect authenticated routes

title: Employee ID Layout Component
description: Create reusable React component for rendering ID card layout (front and back). Component used for both PDF generation and preview. Includes all fields and proper styling.
tests: Component renders ID front with all required fields
Component renders ID back with QR code
Component handles missing photo gracefully
Component applies school branding correctly
Component is responsive and printable
Component validates required props

title: SIS Sync Status Dashboard
description: Create admin dashboard showing SIS sync status: last sync time, sync errors, employees pending sync, sync statistics. Helps admins monitor data freshness.
tests: Dashboard displays last sync timestamp
Dashboard shows sync errors if any occurred
Dashboard lists employees with sync issues
Dashboard shows sync statistics (total, active, inactive)
Dashboard is only accessible to admins
Dashboard refreshes sync status on demand

title: Employee ID Expiration Notifications
description: Implement system to identify and notify admins of upcoming ID expirations (e.g., within 30 days). Display warnings in admin interface and optionally send email alerts.
tests: System identifies IDs expiring within 30 days
Expiring IDs are displayed in admin interface
Warning messages are clear and actionable
Expiration calculation uses correct date logic
Already expired IDs are identified separately
Email notifications can be sent (optional)

title: Integration Tests for Full ID Workflow
description: Create end-to-end integration tests covering complete ID workflow: SIS sync → employee login → view ID → admin uploads photo → generate PDF → verify via QR code.
tests: E2E test: Employee can complete full ID workflow
E2E test: Admin can manage employee ID end-to-end
E2E test: Public verification works from QR code scan
E2E test: Expiration logic works in real scenarios
All integration tests pass reliably
Tests cover error scenarios as well as happy paths

title: FACTS Profile Picture Integration with Caching
description: When displaying employee photos, if no uploaded photo exists, fetch the profile picture from the FACTS API endpoint /People/{personId}/ProfilePicture (using sisEmployeeId as personId). Cache the fetched photo in local storage and update the EmployeeID.photoUrl field. If the FACTS API call fails, show a "No Photo" placeholder. Uploaded photos always take precedence over FACTS photos.
tests: Unit: Service function fetches profile picture from FACTS API using correct personId
Unit: Service function handles FACTS API errors (404, 500, network) gracefully and returns null
Unit: Service function caches fetched FACTS photo to storage and updates EmployeeID record
Unit: Photo fetching logic prioritizes uploaded photo over FACTS photo
E2E: Employee ID displays FACTS photo when no uploaded photo exists
E2E: Employee ID displays uploaded photo when both uploaded and FACTS photos exist
E2E: Employee ID shows "No Photo" placeholder when FACTS API returns 404 or error
Manual: Verify FACTS photos are cached and don't require repeated API calls
